title: ls/ready row: drop label-count column, add type column
body:
# Goal

Rework `jjf ls`'s default plain-text row to drop the
near-useless `<labels>L` column and add the type column.
Compare current to target:

```
# current
cc2fa96  open  1L  host-asterinas-migrate: …
ec5a0a8  open  2L  Epic: host-asterinas-workspace: …

# target
cc2fa96  open  feature  host-asterinas-migrate: …
ec5a0a8  open  epic     Epic: host-asterinas-workspace: …
```

# Why

Beads' `bd list` (the inspiration) doesn't put labels in
the default row at all — only the long-form output lists
them, where it shows the actual values, not a count. A
count tells you almost nothing; the values would, but
they overflow the row on heavily-labeled issues. Type
is the column scanners actually want — "is this a bug?
an epic?" — and it's already a first-class field
(`IssueType` per `crates/jjf-storage/src/record.rs:298`),
just unused in the row today.

# Source

- `crates/jjf/src/main.rs:3252-3334` — `run_ls` and its
  row formatter. The format string at line ~3316 is:
  `"{id}\t{status}\t{n}L\t{title}"`. Change to:
  `"{id}\t{status}\t{type}\t{title}"`.
- `crates/jjf-storage/src/record.rs:298-326` — `IssueType`
  enum and `as_str()` wire spelling. Use `as_str()` for
  the column value verbatim.

# Scope

- Plain-text row only. `ls --json` shape unchanged.
- `ls --slug` and the other filter flags unchanged.
- `ready`'s row format mirrors `ls`'s today; keep them in
  sync — `ready`'s row should also drop the label count
  and add type.

# Out of scope

- Adding a `--long` mode (the row-format-as-flag pattern
  beads uses with `--pretty` / `--long` / `--flat`). One
  format is fine for v1; revisit if the row gets cramped
  again.
- Adding labels in any form. They're available via
  `--json | jq` and via `jjf show`.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `jjf ls` default row is `<id>\t<status>\t<type>\t<title>`.
- [ ] `jjf ready` matches the same row format (mirror change).
- [ ] `--json` envelope shape unchanged.
- [ ] Existing tests that asserted on the `2L` label-count
      column update to the new format. Don't loosen the
      assertions; tighten them — assert the new column is
      exactly the type's wire spelling.
- [ ] `Unspecified` renders as `unspecified` (the type's
      `as_str` value), matching how the JSON field already
      reports it.

# Cross-refs

- The asymmetry was surfaced 2026-06-27 while comparing
  to beads' `bd list` formatter at
  `reference/beads/cmd/bd/list_format.go:42-72`.
